personal.name: Mitchell Schwarzer
personal.title: Architectural & Urban Historian
personal.subtitle: Professor Emeritus of Art, Architecture, and Urban History at the California College of the Arts (CCA)
personal.bio: Mitchell Schwarzer is a prominent American historian, author, and Professor Emeritus at the California College of the Arts (CCA). His research and teaching interests focus on architecture, urbanism, landscape, infrastructure, and art history. He is widely recognized for his work on urban history and preservation, particularly regarding the San Francisco Bay Area and Oakland. His books include 'Hella Town: Oakland's History of Development and Disruption' (2021), 'Zoomscape: Architecture in Motion and Media' (2004), and 'German Architectural Theory and the Search for Modern Identity' (1995).

education.details: Dissertation: 'Adolf Loos and Theories of the Practical Arts.'

professional_experience.company: San Francisco Department of City Planning
professional_experience.title: City Planner
professional_experience.details: Historic Preservation Element of the Master Plan (1989); South End Warehouse Historic District (1990). Preservation statutes for San Francisco Planning Code (1986) and Downtown Plan (1985). Urban design analyses for: Rincon Hill Plan (1985); North of Market Plan (1985); Chinatown Plan (1986); and Neighborhood Commercial Rezoning Study (1985).

professional_experience.title: City Planner
professional_experience.details: Evaluations of buildings and districts for municipal Master Plans, Environmental Impact Reports, and the National Register of Historic Places.

books.title: Hella Town: Oakland’s History of Development and Disruption
books.description: A comprehensive narrative history of Oakland's built environment, detailing how transportation improvements, systemic racism, and regional competition have shaped the city. It explores how past political and economic decisions continue to influence modern-day urban conditions, such as housing costs and social inequality.

books.title: Architecture of the San Francisco Bay Area: History and Guide
books.description: An essential architectural history and fieldwork guide of the San Francisco Bay Area, tracing key movements, architects, and landmark structures that define the region's unique urban landscape.

books.title: Zoomscape: Architecture in Motion and Media
books.description: An exploration of how the perception of architecture has been fundamentally transformed by modern speed (trains, cars, planes) and media (photography, film, television), defining the dynamic visual environment of the 'zoomscape'.

books.title: Architecture and Design: San Francisco
books.description: A visual guide detailing the urban history, styles, and significant building achievements of San Francisco.

books.title: German Architectural Theory and the Search for Modern Identity
books.description: The first comprehensive English overview of architectural theory debates in nineteenth-century Austria and Germany, detailing how industrialization and the search for national identity shaped modern design principles.
